label: The Gold Economy & Regulated Retail Cooling

newsletter.headline: Gold loans tripled while credit cards flatlined — two products, one policy
newsletter.summary: RBI's unsecured credit tightening (Nov 2023) is simultaneously suppressing credit cards and redirecting household demand toward gold-backed lending. The Jan 2025→Jan 2026 comparison makes both movements unambiguous.

newsletter.headline: MSME YoY credit growth tripled in one year — this is now confirmed structural
newsletter.summary: Micro & Small enterprise YoY growth jumped from 9.6% (Jan 2025) to 31.2% (Jan 2026). PSL Micro & Small corroborates at +25.6% FY. Two independent series pointing the same direction: UDYAM + GST have crossed the underwriting threshold.

newsletter.headline: All Engineering up 62% in 2 years — PLI drawdowns are now real banking flows
newsletter.summary: PLI scheme recipients approved in 2022-23 entered the investment phase in FY25-26. ₹1.96L Cr → ₹2.32L Cr → ₹3.16L Cr across three Feb data points. The announcement phase is over — credit drawdowns are happening.

newsletter.headline: +38% PSL housing growth is a classification event, not organic lending
newsletter.summary: The Oct 2024 RBI revision raised PSL housing limits (metro: ₹35L→₹45L). Loans already on books that previously exceeded the threshold now qualify. The ₹2.87L Cr appearing as credit growth is reclassification, not new lending. Context mandatory when citing.

label: The Infrastructure Plateau & Next Cycle Pivot

newsletter.headline: Infrastructure credit decelerating — banks need to pivot to the next capex wave
newsletter.summary: Infrastructure at ₹14.44L Cr and 32.5% of industry credit is growing at only +5.8% FY. The FY22-24 capex wave projects are now in operations, repaying not drawing down. Green hydrogen, data centers, and renewable energy are the next wave — not yet at drawdown scale.

newsletter.headline: Bank credit added ₹44L Cr in two years — FY26 accelerating above FY25
newsletter.summary: Total bank credit: ₹160.4L Cr (Jan 2024) → ₹178.7L Cr (Jan 2025) → ₹204.8L Cr (Jan 2026). YoY growth lifted from 11.4% to 14.6%. FY26 tracking at 13.8% vs FY25's 11.0% — structural acceleration, not base effect.
